data.title: China-Thailand youth exchange program launched in Bangkok with electric scooters featuring panda mascot "Upanda".
data.description: Today in Bangkok, the "Boundless Creativity • Youth Link" China–Thailand Youth Cultural Exchange Event kicked off with a colorful parade of electric scooters adorned with the Chinese giant panda mascot "Upanda." This event, organized by LUYUAN, celebrates cultural exchange between China and Thailand. The parade attracted a crowd of onlookers in Bangkok as the fleet of electric scooters, representing youth creativity, paraded through the streets.

data.title: Mitsubishi Electric invests in ADT Technology Service (Suzhou) in China. Mitsubishi Electric Intelligent Manufacturing Tech acquires a stake in ADTTech, a Suzhou-based software developer.
data.description: In Tokyo, Mitsubishi Electric Corporation (TOKYO: 6503) shared news of its subsidiary, Mitsubishi Electric Intelligent Manufacturing Technology (China) Group Co., Ltd., acquiring a stake in ADT Technology Service (Suzhou) Co., Ltd. based in Suzhou, China. The subsidiary had previously entered a collaboration agreement with ADT Tech, a software developer specializing in production planning, manufacturing management, and quality control solutions. This move signifies a strategic partnership between the companies for further development and innovation in the industry.

data.title: English version of Fantôme Rapport launches on Jan 16, 2026 in Osaka, Japan. A mysterious horror romance set on a ghostly luxury liner.
data.description: In Osaka, Japan, an English version of Fantôme Rapport will be released on January 16, 2026, according to a recent announcement. This anime, categorized as a romantic horror mystery, is set on a haunted luxury liner where interpersonal connections play a crucial role in determining one's destiny.

data.title: Ransomware DeadLock utilizes Polygon smart contracts to conceal its activities, rotating proxy addresses to evade detection.
data.description: A cybersecurity firm named Group-IB has discovered a ransomware called DeadLock that is using Polygon smart contracts to change proxy addresses, making it difficult to be shut down.

data.title: Peter Schiff advises Iranians facing currency collapse to choose gold-backed cryptocurrencies over Bitcoin due to crisis in Iran.
data.description: Economist Peter Schiff expressed his preference for a gold-backed cryptocurrency over Bitcoin during a crisis situation similar to the one in Iran. In an interview, Schiff stated that if he needed to make anonymous transactions through an app in Iran due to the currency crisis and internet restrictions, he would opt for tokenized gold over Bitcoin. He emphasized his trust in a cryptocurrency supported by tangible assets like gold, contrasting it with Bitcoin which he claimed is backed by nothing. Schiff suggested that using a stablecoin would be the most viable option in Iran, noting the availability of several options in the market. To read the full story, visit Benzinga.com.
